summary: FPV drones and loitering munitions are being used to destroy a wide range of targets, including Russian TOS-1A MLRS systems, motorcycles used in assaults, and even self-propelled artillery like the Bogdan. Ukrainian forces have repelled Russian assaults in Zaporizhzhia using FPV drones, while Russian operators have captured intact Ukrainian "Shark-M" UAVs after deploying their parachutes via kinetic intercept. The "Rubicon" Center reports over 24,000 episodes of enemy target destruction, with unmanned systems comprising 36.7% of all destroyed targets, and continues to conduct operations across multiple fronts including Donbas, Krasny Liman, Belgorod, and Sumy. Ukrainian infantry rely heavily on drone operators for close support, and both sides are adapting to electronic warfare, with some units using drone detectors to survive FPV attacks.
